workflow_name: Daily NYC Weather Summary with Freezing Alert
description: Fetches daily weather data for New York City from Open-Meteo each morning, highlights freezing temperatures prominently, and delivers a formatted summary via webhook output. Email delivery is out of scope for this implementation; the downstream webhook endpoint is responsible for any email conversion.

trigger.description: Runs every morning at 7:00 AM New York time (America/New_York). n8n server MUST have GENERIC_TIMEZONE=America/New_York set, OR the cron expression must be adjusted to UTC equivalent. The cron expression '0 7 * * *' is interpreted in the server's system timezone.

steps.name: Schedule Daily Morning Trigger
steps.description: Triggers the workflow every day at 7:00 AM. IMPORTANT: n8n interprets cron expressions in the server's system timezone. Set the n8n environment variable GENERIC_TIMEZONE=America/New_York to ensure this fires at 7:00 AM New York time. If the server runs in UTC, '0 7 * * *' fires at 7:00 AM UTC (2–3 AM New York time).
steps.error_handling: No action needed; cron triggers are managed by n8n scheduler.

steps.name: Fetch NYC Weather from Open-Meteo
steps.description: Calls the Open-Meteo API to get current weather data for New York City including temperature (Fahrenheit), weather code, wind speed, and daily min/max temperature plus precipitation sum for a complete morning briefing. Note: the 'daily' parameter is sent as a single comma-separated string value, which is what Open-Meteo expects. Verify in development that n8n does not double-encode the commas.
steps.error_handling: Retry up to 2 times with 1s delay; if all retries fail, stop workflow and log error. Note: Open-Meteo may return HTTP 200 with error:true in the JSON body for application-level errors — these are caught in step_3.

steps.name: Parse, Normalize, and Build Weather Message
steps.description: Validates the API response shape (including Open-Meteo application-level errors), extracts current and daily weather fields with null guards, maps the WMO weather code to a human-readable condition string, computes a freezing flag, and builds the complete subject/body/alertLevel output in one step.
steps.error_handling: Throws a descriptive error if: Open-Meteo returns error:true, current_weather is missing, any required current_weather field is null/undefined, or the time field has an unexpected format. If any parsing fails, stop workflow and log the raw API response for debugging.

steps.name: Validate Webhook URL Configuration
steps.description: Pre-flight check that validates WEATHER_WEBHOOK_URL is configured, non-empty, and appears to be a valid URL before attempting delivery. Throws a descriptive error early if misconfigured, preventing an opaque failure in the HTTP Request node.

output_shape.passthrough: All fields from step_3 are passed through unchanged

steps.error_handling: Throws a descriptive configuration error if WEATHER_WEBHOOK_URL is missing or malformed. Workflow stops immediately with a clear message.

steps.name: Send Weather Summary via Webhook POST
steps.description: Delivers the formatted weather summary (freezing alert or normal) to a downstream webhook endpoint (e.g., Slack, email relay, or notification service). URL must be configured via environment variable WEATHER_WEBHOOK_URL before deployment. Bearer token must be configured via environment variable WEATHER_WEBHOOK_TOKEN. Email delivery is out of scope; the downstream webhook service handles any email conversion.
steps.error_handling: Retry up to 2 times with 1s delay; if all retries fail, log the failure with the message payload for manual review. A global error workflow should fire to notify the operator of delivery failure.

error_handling.on_workflow_failure: Log error details including step ID, timestamp, and error message. Trigger n8n error workflow to send a fallback operator alert if the weather fetch or delivery fails.

output.destination: Webhook POST to downstream notification endpoint configured via WEATHER_WEBHOOK_URL environment variable (configurable: Slack, email relay, PagerDuty, etc.). Email delivery is out of scope for this workflow — the downstream webhook service is responsible for any email conversion.
output.format: JSON payload with subject, body (plain text formatted), alertLevel, temperature, condition, windspeed, dateStr, tempMax, tempMin, precipSum
output.description: A human-readable daily weather summary for NYC, with freezing days flagged prominently via subject line emoji, alert level field, and warning body text. Includes daily high/low and precipitation for a complete morning briefing.

credentials_needed: WEATHER_WEBHOOK_URL — webhook delivery endpoint URL, stored as n8n environment variable; MUST be set before deployment

WEATHER_WEBHOOK_TOKEN — Bearer token for Authorization header on outbound webhook POST, stored as n8n environment variable (referenced via $env.WEATHER_WEBHOOK_TOKEN). Note: n8n's $credential.* syntax is NOT valid for expression fields; environment variables are used instead.
No API key required for Open-Meteo (free, public API)
security.pii_handling: No PII is collected or transmitted. Weather data is public.
security.data_flow: Open-Meteo API → n8n (in-memory processing) → Downstream webhook. No data is persisted.
security.outbound_auth: All webhook POSTs include an Authorization: Bearer header using the WEATHER_WEBHOOK_TOKEN environment variable to prevent spoofing.
security.timezone_dependency: The cron trigger relies on the n8n server timezone. Set GENERIC_TIMEZONE=America/New_York on the n8n server to ensure 7:00 AM New York local time firing. If the server runs in UTC, the cron expression must be adjusted (e.g., '0 12 * * *' for UTC noon = 7 AM ET winter / 8 AM ET summer — DST must be accounted for manually if not using GENERIC_TIMEZONE).

cost_estimate.notes: Open-Meteo is free with no rate limits for reasonable usage. n8n self-hosted has no per-execution cost. Downstream webhook cost depends on provider (e.g., Slack free tier is sufficient).

test_cases.name: Freezing alert path – temperature exactly at 32°F
Malformed API response – missing current_weather field

simulated_api_response.reason: Parameter latitude is out of range

test_cases.name: Open-Meteo application-level error – HTTP 200 with error:true

simulated_api_response.reason: Cannot initialize WeatherVariable from invalid String value temperature_2m_INVALID for VariableWithoutUnit

expected.notes: The regex /^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}T/ will still match this format so it passes. This test confirms the guard does not over-reject valid ISO variants that begin with the expected date prefix. If a radically different format were returned (e.g., Unix timestamp), it would throw.

test_cases.name: WEATHER_WEBHOOK_URL not configured – pre-flight validation catch

expected.error_message_contains: WEATHER_WEBHOOK_URL environment variable is not set
does not appear to be a valid URL
